evals.prompt: I just started a new Node.js + Postgres + Gemini AI project called Baitorng-v2. It's a 3-day sprint, deadline June 20. Help me set up the context architecture.
evals.expected_output: Generates Router File (≤50 lines), context/AGENT.md (≤100 lines with all 9 sections), context/LAWS.md skeleton, context/SKILL_INDEX.md, .learnings/errors/ folder, .gitignore additions. All 8 deliverables ready.
assertions: AGENT.md contains all 9 required sections including LAST SESSION HANDOFF block

evals.prompt: My Claude Code session is at 40% context and I'm mid-task on a security audit for my chatbot upload endpoint. Haven't committed yet. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Routes to Module 3. Gives clear decision: compress context (not fresh session) since mid-task + no commit. Exact steps: update AGENT.md → fill LAST SESSION HANDOFF → compress. Explains why fresh session would lose work.
assertions: Recommends compress/compact (not fresh session) because mid-task
Lists the pre-compress steps in order: update AGENT.md first, then handoff block
Explains the difference between compress and fresh session

evals.prompt: Should I use the most powerful model or the fastest model for writing unit tests for my upload endpoint?
evals.expected_output: Routes to Module 4. Answers: Tier 1 (Daily Driver / fastest). Explains why: unit tests = mechanical execution = Tier 1. Warns against switching to Tier 2 unnecessarily. Directs to provider docs for current model names.
assertions: Explains why: unit tests are mechanical execution
Does NOT hardcode specific model names
Points to provider docs for current names

evals.prompt: My AGENT.md is 180 lines and the AI agent keeps forgetting what we built last session. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Module 2 (Trim Protocol). Identifies bloat, moves detail to LAWS.md with pointer examples. Keeps: CURRENT STATE, NEXT TASKS, KEY FILES, RUN commands, LAST HANDOFF. Verifies wc -l ≤ 100.
assertions: Identifies what to move vs keep

evals.prompt: I switched from Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI. What file do I use as my Router File now?
evals.expected_output: Routes to Universal Vocabulary table. Answers: AGENTS.md or GEMINI.md for Antigravity CLI. Notes Gemini CLI was deprecated June 18, 2026. Explains Agent Brain (AGENT.md) stays the same across tools.
assertions: Correctly identifies AGENTS.md or GEMINI.md for Antigravity CLI
Explains Agent Brain (context/AGENT.md) is the same regardless of tool
Does not confuse Antigravity CLI Router File with Gemini CLI

evals.prompt: I'm using Kiro from AWS for my new project. How do I set up my context architecture?
evals.expected_output: Routes to Universal Vocabulary + Module 1. Identifies .kiro/steering/*.md as Router File equivalent for Kiro. All other layers (context/AGENT.md, LAWS.md, .learnings/) are the same. Delivers 8-item Output Contract.
assertions: Correctly identifies .kiro/steering/*.md as Kiro's Router File
Response routes through Module 1 with tool-specific Router File note
